stories.headline: AEP threatens PJM, SPP exit over slow generation interconnection
stories.summary: AEP CEO Bill Fehrman told investors May 5 the utility is reviewing whether to leave PJM and SPP, citing interconnection delays as 16 GW of contracted PJM data-center load awaits power.

stories.headline: Xcel-Google deal frames new template for large-load tariff strategy
stories.summary: Xcel Energy's recent Google contract is being held up as a model for utilities pricing data-center load through dedicated large-load tariffs rather than spreading the cost to retail ratepayers.

stories.headline: Duke adds 2.7 GW of contracted data-center load in Q1
stories.summary: Duke Energy reported 2.7 GW of new contracted data-center load in the first quarter, pushing its pipeline higher as Carolinas and Indiana service territories continue to absorb AI-driven demand.

stories.headline: PSEG CEO points to nuclear as answer for NJ load growth
stories.summary: PSEG CEO Ralph LaRossa told analysts nuclear is the cleanest path to meet New Jersey's surging electricity demand, with Salem and Hope Creek anchoring the company's plans.

stories.headline: McCormick tours Beaver Valley nuclear, ties AI buildout to PA generation
stories.summary: Sen. Dave McCormick toured Vistra's 1,872-MW Beaver Valley nuclear plant in Shippingport on Wednesday, calling Pennsylvania's nuclear fleet central to the AI buildout and pushing to expand the state's nuclear footprint.

stories.headline: Shapiro stakes coattails on PA-7 Democratic primary
stories.summary: Gov. Josh Shapiro is going all-in for Bob Brooks ahead of the May 19 PA-7 Democratic primary against Lamont McClure, framing the Mackenzie-rematch race as a test of his pull in the state's marquee 2026 House battleground.

stories.headline: PA Senate Judiciary advances constitutional carry
stories.summary: PA Senate Judiciary, chaired by Sen. Lisa Baker, advanced SB 357 Wednesday, which would let any adult legally able to own a firearm carry concealed without a state-issued permit.

stories.headline: PA House Democrats demand budget offer from Senate GOP
stories.summary: PA House Democrats publicly challenged Senate Republicans on Wednesday to send over a budget proposal, escalating the FY26-27 stalemate as the June 30 deadline approaches.

stories.headline: Republican redistricting wave reshapes 2026 House map
stories.summary: Florida pushed through a four-seat GOP gerrymander, Tennessee may vote on a Cohen-targeted map today, and SCOTUS's Louisiana v. Callais ruling has put as many as 10 Democratic seats in play.

stories.headline: Senate shelves SAVE Act as filibuster fight escalates
stories.summary: Senate GOP leaders are dropping further floor votes on the SAVE America voter-citizenship bill after a vote-a-rama amendment fell short of 50 votes, intensifying base pressure to gut the filibuster.

stories.headline: Mark Kelly v. Hegseth at D.C. Circuit today
stories.summary: A three-judge D.C. Circuit panel hears oral arguments at 9:30 a.m. on the Pentagon's appeal of the injunction blocking Hegseth from punishing Sen. Mark Kelly; PA Reps. Houlahan and Deluzio joined an amicus brief backing Kelly.

stories.headline: Rubio meets Pope Leo XIV amid Vatican-White House rift
stories.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio sits down with Pope Leo XIV today after Trump's Truth Social attacks and a 'Jesus meme' drove U.S.-Vatican relations to historic lows; Rubio is downplaying the trip as routine.

stories.headline: Mystery PAC drops $500K in PA-7 Democratic primary
stories.summary: Lead Left PAC, a newly registered super PAC, is spending $500,000 on TV in PA-7 to boost Lamont McClure over DCCC-backed Bob Brooks ahead of the May 19 Democratic primary.

stories.headline: Skenes shuts down D-backs as Pirates win 1-0; Reynolds gets 1,000th hit
stories.summary: Paul Skenes threw 8 innings of two-hit ball with 7 strikeouts and Brandon Lowe's 435-foot solo HR was the only run as Pittsburgh beat Arizona 1-0 Wednesday; Bryan Reynolds notched his 1,000th career hit.

stories.headline: Penguins reportedly preparing to move on from Malkin
stories.summary: GM Kyle Dubas is set to tell Evgeni Malkin's camp this week the Penguins plan to move on, with the 39-year-old center expected to test free agency July 1 after his $6.1M AAV deal expires.

stories.headline: Steelers OTAs set to begin May 18; draft class drawing scrutiny
stories.summary: Pittsburgh's OTA dates were finalized this week with the first session May 18, while early reviews of the 10-pick 2026 draft class — led by 1st-round OT Max Iheanachor — drew mixed reactions.
